middleware.description: Resolves the caller's active Region for THIS request. Reads (in order): (1) `X-Region-Id` header when the caller's active tenant has a Region on that slug or ULID AND the Region's status is 'active' (paused / archived rejected on booking-adjacent routes; permitted on read-only routes with a warning header); (2) DefaultRegionResolver → the tenant's Region where is_default=true. Sets `Request::activeRegion()` + writes to the `RequestContext.region_id` slot that downstream middleware (subscription pricing, finance tax calc) reads. Runs AFTER `tenant.resolve` (needs tenant_id) but BEFORE any domain policy check that consults `activeRegion()`.

responses.condition: X-Region-Id header points at a Region in a different tenant
responses.description: Security-relevant — a forged header attempting to reach another tenant's Region. Logged with the tenant + user + attempted region + IP.

responses.condition: X-Region-Id header points at a paused Region on a booking-adjacent route (POST /bookings, POST /memberships)
X-Region-Id header points at an archived Region
responses.description: Region exists but has been archived. Response includes the current default region's id as a hint.

responses.condition: No X-Region-Id header AND tenant has no default Region
responses.description: System-invariant violation — every tenant should have a default. The reconciler will alert; consumers should retry once the invariant is restored.
